1.1: Aleph Just as the city sat alone, she was great among the nations! She was like a widow; she was great among the nations. The Head of cities was paying taxes!
1.2: Beth She weeps in the night and her tears run down on her cheeks and there is no comforter for her from all her lovers. All her lovers have been false to her and have become her enemies
1.3: Gimel Yehuda is made captive from bondage and from much slavery. She dwelt among the nations and she has not found rest for herself. All her pursuers overtook her in the place of suffering
1.4: Daleth The ways of Zion are in mourning for lack of those coming to the feast. All her gates are deserted, and her Priests groan, and her virgins are humiliated, and she has made herself bitter
1.5: He They have been her oppressors, for the Ruler and her enemies have been quiet, because LORD JEHOVAH humbled her, because of the multitude of her sins, and her children went into captivity in front of the oppressor
1.6: Waw And all her Nobles went out from the daughter of Zion. The Rulers were like stags and they found no pasture, and they went without power before the persecutor
1.7: Zayin Jerusalem has remembered the days of her oppression and her chastisement, and all her pleasant things from the first days when her people fell into the hand of the oppressor, and there was no helper for her. Her oppressors saw her and they laughed at her ruin
1.8: Kheth Jerusalem has sinned a sin, because of this she has become an abomination. All who honored her despise her, because they saw her nakedness; also she has groaned and she has turned her back
1.9: Teth Her defilement is in her skirts, and she did not remember her end, and her glory has come down and there was no comforter for her. See, LORD JEHOVAH, my oppression, for the enemy is exalted
1.10: Yodh The oppressor spread his hands over all her pleasures, and she saw the Gentiles who entered your sanctuary, whom you have commanded that they shall not enter your assembly
1.11: Kaph All the people groan and seek bread. They have given their desirable things for food to restore the soul. See, LORD JEHOVAH, and look, for I have been despised!
1.12: Lamed Is it nothing to all you passersby on the street? Be wise and see if there is pain as my pain that LORD JEHOVAH made for me, and LORD JEHOVAH humiliated me in the day of the heat of his wrath
1.13: Mem From the high place he sent fire into my bones, and he brought me down. He spread a net for my feet and he turned me backward and gave me to the sword, and every day I am wretched
1.14: Nun My sins have awakened against me and by its hands its yokes are bound on my neck, and my power became weak, and LORD JEHOVAH has delivered me into the hands of him whose power I cannot match
1.15: Samekh LORD JEHOVAH has subdued all my mighty ones within me. He has called against me a feast day to destroy my young men. LORD JEHOVAH has trodden the winepress the virgin daughter of Yehuda
1.16: Ayin For these things I weep and my eyes run down with waters because the Comforter restoring my soul is far from me. My children have become sycamores, because the enemy has prevailed
1.17: Pe Zion has stretched out her hands and there is no comforter for her. LORD JEHOVAH has commanded against Yaqob and his oppressors around him. Jerusalem has become an abomination in their midst
1.18: Tsadhe Righteous is LORD JEHOVAH against whom I have rebelled. Hear, all you nations, and see my sorrow! My virgins and my young men have gone into captivity!
1.19: Qoph I cried to my friends and they betrayed me, and my Elders within have come to an end, for they searched food for themselves to restore their soul, and they did not find
1.20: Resh See, LORD JEHOVAH, that I have lost heart myself and my bowels are troubled! My heart is cast down within me, for I have rebelled to rebel. The sword from outside has destroyed and death killed those in the house
1.21: Shin Hear that I am groaning and there is no comforter for me. All my enemies have heard my misery and they rejoiced in what you have done to me. Bring the day that you called and they shall be like me
1.22: Tau Bring in all their evil before you strike them, just as you struck me for all my sins, because my groanings are many, and my heart is wretched!

2.1: Aleph How LORD JEHOVAH has overclouded the daughter of Zion in his wrath! He cast the glory of Israel from Heaven to the Earth, and he has not remembered the footstool of his feet in the day of the heat of his wrath!
2.2: Beth LORD JEHOVAH has drowned and has not pitied the whole sheepfold of Yaqob. He has cast down in his anger the fortress city of the house of Yehuda. He cast on the ground her slain, her Kings and her Princes
2.3: Gimel He has cut off in the heat of his wrath all the horn of Israel. He has turned his right hand to his back from before the enemy and he set fire in Yaqob and a flame consumed his hands
2.4: Daleth He fills his bow like the oppressor. He raised his right hand as an enemy and he killed every lust of eyes in the tent of the house of Zion and he poured out his anger as fire
2.5: He LORD JEHOVAH was as an enemy and he drowned Israel, and he sank all his palaces and destroyed his surroundings and he increased in the daughter of Yehuda a byword and wailings
2.6: Waw And he has uprooted his tabernacle like a garden and has destroyed his feasts. LORD JEHOVAH has made the feast and the Sabbath to be forgotten from Zion, and he despised the King and the Priests in the heat of his wrath
2.7: Zayin LORD JEHOVAH has forgotten his altar. He has despised his holy place. He has delivered the walls of his palaces into the hand of the enemy. They made noise in the house of LORD JEHOVAH like that in the day of a festival
2.8: Kheth LORD JEHOVAH has planned to destroy the walls of the house of Zion. He cast a cord and will not return his hand from sinking her, and he put his army in mourning, and its walls they laid waste together
2.9: Teth Her gates have sunk into the ground, and they destroyed and broke her bars. Her Kings and her Princes are among the Gentiles. There is no Law, also her Prophets have not found a vision from LORD JEHOVAH
2.10: Yodh They have dwelt in the land and the Elders of the house of Zion kept silent. They cast dust on their heads and they put on sackcloth. The virgins of Jerusalem bend their heads to the ground
2.11: Kaph My eyes grew dark with tears and my bowels were troubled. My glory is cast down to the ground because of the ruin of the house of my people, when the children and the babies were afflicted in the streets of the city
2.12: Lamed To their mothers they say: “Where is the grain and the wine and the oil?”, when they were afflicted as the slain in the streets of the city, when their souls are poured out into the bosom of their mothers
2.13: Mem What shall I testify to you, and to whom shall I liken you, daughter of Jerusalem? With whom shall I compare you and comfort you, virgin daughter of Zion, for your breach is great as the sea? Who shall close it up?
2.14: Nun Your Prophets have seen falsehood and deception for you, and they have not laid bare for you concerning your sins that you would return to God and I would turn your captivity, and they saw for you lying and deceptive prophecies
2.15: Samekh All passersby on the road strike their hands over you. They hiss and they shake their heads at the daughter of Jerusalem and they say: “Is this the city which they have been saying is the perfection of beauty, and the joy of all the Earth?”
2.16: Ayin LORD JEHOVAH has done the thing that he planned and he has fulfilled his word, as he commanded from the first days. He has destroyed her and he had no pity, and the enemy rejoiced over you and he lifted up the horn of your oppressors
2.17: Pe All your enemies have opened their mouths against you. They hissed and they gnashed their teeth and they were saying: ‘We have devoured her! Surely this is the day that we waited for! We have found it and we see it!’
2.18: Tsadhe Their heart cried to LORD JEHOVAH of the walls of the daughter of Zion. Make flow as a torrent of tears by day and by night! You shall not be given refreshing for yourselves, and the pupil of your eye shall not cease!
2.19: Qoph Arise, sing in the night at first watch, and pour out your heart like waters before the face of LORD JEHOVAH! Raise to him your hands for the souls of your little children who are suffering with hunger at the top of all the streets!
2.20: Resh See, LORD JEHOVAH, and look to whom you have done thus, as women shall eat their offspring the children who are dashed to pieces! Yes, Priest and Prophet shall be killed in the holy place of LORD JEHOVAH
2.21: Shin Children and Elders sleep on the ground in the street. My virgins and my young men fell by the sword. You have killed in the day of your wrath! You have slaughtered and you showed no pity!
2.22: Tau You called my enemies from around me as in the day of a feast and there was no fugitive or survivor in the day of the wrath of LORD JEHOVAH. Whomever I have carried or raised, my enemies have consumed!

3.1: Aleph God The Mighty One saw my oppression with the rod of his wrath
3.2: He led me and he walked me in darkness and not in the light
3.3: However he shall turn and he shall return his hand against me all day
3.4: Beth He has worn out my skin and my flesh and he has broken my bones
3.5: He has built up against me and bitterness and desolation surrounded me*
3.6: In darkness he made me dwell like the dead for eternity
3.7: Gimel He has shut in my presence that I would not come out, and he strengthened my chains
3.8: Even if I shall seek and pray, he does not hear my prayer .
3.9: He hedged my ways in with thorns and turned my steps aside
3.10: Daleth For a wolf was lying in wait for me and a lion in a secret place
3.11: My roads he has made crooked; he has torn me to pieces and he has put me into destruction
3.12: He filled his bow and he stood me up as the mark for an arrow
3.13: He Behold, he has brought his arrows into my kidneys
3.14: I have been a mockery among all the nations and their chant all day
3.15: He has filled me with bitterness, and he made me drunk with wormwood
3.16: Waw And he broke my teeth with a stone and he covered me in ashes
3.17: And my soul has been led astray from peace and I have forgotten good
3.18: And I said: "My victory and my hope are destroyed from LORD JEHOVAH!”
3.19: Zayin Remember my oppression and my chastisement of bitterness and of wormwood!
3.20: Remember and return my soul to me!
3.21: This I shall set on my heart. Because of this, I shall wait for him
3.22: Kheth The kindness of LORD JEHOVAH that does not fail and his mercies that do not end
3.23: In the newness of the morning, great is your faithfulness , LORD JEHOVAH!
3.24: My soul has said: “He is my portion”; because of this, I shall wait for him
3.25: Teth LORD JEHOVAH is good to him who waits for him, and to the soul that seeks him
3.26: It is good for him who hopes in truth for the redemption of LORD JEHOVAH
3.27: It is good for a man when he will take up your yoke in his youth
3.28: Yodh He will sit alone and he will be silent, because he has taken your yoke upon himself
3.29: He will put his mouth into the dust, because there is hope
3.30: He will give his cheek to him who hits him, and he will be filled with reproach
3.31: Kaph Because LORD JEHOVAH does not forget for eternity
3.32: But he is lowly and he shows love according to the multitude of his compassions
3.33: Because he has not answered from his heart, and he humbles the children of mighty men
3.34: Lamed To subdue under his feet all the prisoners of Earth
3.35: To incline the judgment of a man toward the face of The Highest
3.36: LORD JEHOVAH does not countenance condemning a son of man in his cause
3.37: Mem Who is this that speaks and it was what LORD JEHOVAH has not commanded?
3.38: From the mouth of The Exalted One, evil and good do not come out!
3.39: Why does a son of man and a man who lives think much upon his sins?
3.40: Nun We shall search our ways and we shall examine and we shall be converted to LORD JEHOVAH
3.41: We shall lift up our hearts on our hands to God in Heaven!
3.42: We have done evil and we have rebelled and you did not forgive
3.43: Samekh You have overshadowed in your anger and you pursued us and you killed and you did not pity
3.44: You covered yourself up with your cloud and you put away our prayer
3.45: You have caused us to be uprooted and rejected among the nations
3.46: Ayin My eyes shed tears and are not still, from which there is no relief
3.47: Until LORD JEHOVAH will gaze and will see from Heaven
3.48: My eye was troubled by my soul for all the daughters of my cities
3.49: Pe All my enemies have opened their mouths against me
3.50: Fear and shaking was ours, the pit and ruin
3.51: My eyes made streams of waters flow for the ruin of the daughter of my people
3.52: Tsadhe My enemies hunted me freely like a bird
3.53: They ended my life in a pit; they cast stones at me
3.54: The waters flowed above my head. I said, “I am taken far away!”
3.55: Qoph I cried your name, LORD JEHOVAH, from the pit below
3.56: You heard my voice. “Do not incline your ear, but free me and save me!”
3.57: You came in the day that I called you and you said to me: “Do not be afraid!”
3.58: Resh You judged, LORD JEHOVAH, the cause of my soul, and you saved my life!
3.59: You have seen, LORD JEHOVAH, my affliction! You decided my judgment!
3.60: You have seen all their vengeance, and all that they have devised against me
3.61: Shin You have heard their reproach, LORD JEHOVAH, and all that they have devised against me
3.62: The lips of those that stood and schemed against me all day
3.63: Their sitting and their rising I understand from their scheme
3.64: Tau Return to them their payment, LORD JEHOVAH, according to the work of their hands
3.65: Give them grief of heart, and your affliction to them
3.66: You shall pursue, and in your passion you will destroy them from under your heavens, LORD JEHOVAH

4.1: Aleph How the fine gold is rejected and the beautiful dyed garment departed, and the stones of holiness are cast out at the head of all the streets!
4.2: Beth The precious children of Zion who are better than costly stones have been considered just as vessels of earthen work from the hands of a potter!
4.3: Gimel They show their breasts like jackals and they nurse their whelps. The daughter of my people has become a disease for which there is no remedy, and like an ostrich in the wilderness
4.4: Daleth The tongue of the infant is stuck to the roof of his palate in thirst. Children asked for bread and there is none who breaks bread, giving it to them
4.5: He Those who were eating delicacies, behold, they are lying in the streets. They who were raised in scarlet, behold, they are sleeping on dunghills!
4.6: Waw And greater is the evil of the daughter of my people than the sin of Sadom that was overthrown suddenly, and there was no labor in her hands
4.7: Zayin Her Nazarites were purer than snow and they were whiter than the milk of their flocks. Their bones are more red than red sardius, and their bodies are of sapphire
4.8: Kheth Their faces are darker than coal and they are not recognized in the streets. Their skin on their bones is shriveled up; it is dried up and it is like wood
4.9: Teth The slain of the sword are better than those killed by famine, for they waste away like those who are wounded and are thrown out in a field
4.10: Yodh The hands of loving women boiled their children, and they were food for them in the breaking of the daughter of my people
4.11: Kaph LORD JEHOVAH has fulfilled his anger and he poured out the heat of his wrath, and he set fire in Zion, and flame devoured her foundations
4.12: Lamed The Kings of the Earth and all the inhabitants of the world did not believe that the enemy and the oppressor comes and enters the gates of Jerusalem
4.13: Mem Because of the sin of her Prophets and the evil of her Priests who shed the blood of the righteous ones within her
4.14: Nun Her Princes wandered in her streets and they wallowed in blood, and they were unable to touch their garments
4.15: Samekh Separate from them and call them defiled. Depart and do not touch them, because they have provoked anger also, and they are afflicted. Among the nations they said: "They will not continue to dwell anymore"
4.16: Ayin While we wait, our eyes grow dark because of our help! They gazed to gaze for nothing to a nation that does not save!
4.17: Pe The Person of LORD JEHOVAH divided them, and he will not continue to gaze at them. Do not accept the persons of the Priests, and to the Elders do not show affection
4.18: Tsadhe They hunted for those broken in pieces, those walking in the streets. Our end has come; our days are full, because our time has come!
4.19: Qoph Our pursuers have been swifter than the eagles of Heaven! On the mountains they chased us and in the wilderness they ambushed us!
4.20: Resh The Spirit of our nostrils, The Messiah of LORD JEHOVAH, was seized in their pits, he of whom they were saying: “In his shade we shall live among the nations!”
4.21: Shin Dance and rejoice, daughter of Edum, who dwells in the land of Uts. Also to you the cup shall come and you shall be drunk and you shall be afflicted
4.22: Tau Your sins are consumed, daughter of Zion! He will continue your captivity no more! Your violations have been sentenced, daughter of Edum! Your sins have been uncovered!

5.1: Remember, LORD JEHOVAH, what happened to us! Look, and see our reproach!
5.2: Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers and our houses to foreigners
5.3: We were orphans without a father, and our mothers were as widows
5.4: We are made to drink our water for money, and our wood is for a price
5.5: They came for our necks; we were persecuted and we labor and we have no rest
5.6: The Egyptians gave a hand, and the Assyrians, to supply bread
5.7: Our fathers sinned and they are not, and we carry their sins
5.8: Servants are authorized over us and there is none who saves us from their hands
5.9: Into our soul we will put bread in the presence of the sword in the wilderness
5.10: Our skins were burned as by a furnace and they became pale in the wretchedness of famine
5.11: Women in Zion were violated and virgins in the villages of Judea
5.12: Princes were hanged by their hands and they dishonored the persons of the Elders
5.13: The young men ground meal with the millstone and boys stumbled with wood
5.14: They removed Elders from the gate and young men from their joy
5.15: The joy of our hearts has ceased. Our joy has turned into grieving
5.16: The crown of our head has fallen! Woe to us, because we have sinned!
5.17: Because of this, behold, our heart grieves, and because of this, behold, our eyes are darkened!
5.18: Because of the Mountain of Zion that is desolate, and foxes walk in it
5.19: And you, LORD JEHOVAH, you are to eternity, and your throne, forever and ever!
5.20: You shall not leave us for eternity, nor forget us for the length of days!
5.21: Convert us, LORD JEHOVAH, to you, and we shall be converted. Make our days new as those from before!
5.22: Because you have rejected us, and you have been very angry against us.
